title: saussurea kai-montana var. minor
intro: Saussurea kai-montana var. minor, also known as the Tibetan thangka or saussurea root, is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects, which help to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of various respiratory issues. Saussurea kai-montana var. minor is often used in herbal preparations, including decoctions and infusions, which are consumed as teas to harness its medicinal properties.
